div1.entryFree:
Reflect, 1) to throw back light: two glasses . . . and now no more r. Ven. 1130.
2) to shine: whether it is that she --s so bright, Lucr. 376. there were crept, as 'twere in scorn of eyes, --ing gems, R3 I, 4, 31. whose virtues will r. on Rome as Titan's rays on earth, Tit. I, 226. Nearly == to look: he is one to whose kindnesses I am most infinitely tied. R. upon him accordingly, Cymb. I, 6, 24. Transitively: r. I not on thy baseness court-contempt? Wint. IV, 4, 758.
